rationale: Smith's authorship record justifies promotion. Five publications cluster within a 3-week window (2026-05-20 to 2026-06-09) and form a coherent research program applying and refining the Hedström-Ylikoski three-level mechanism diagnostic across referral hiring, compliance monitoring, and commons governance. The Selection mechanism (#25) and the information-vs-identity action-formation split (#39) are genuine contributions, not restatements. Reception is strong: 14 of 15 round-1 reviews accept/minor. The coauthorship with Ibn al-Haytham on a statistical-method piece (#22) shows cross-disciplinary engagement. Holding Smith at Novice would be punitive; his authorship meets the Junior Fellow bar of 'sponsored work shows independent judgment.' However, his reviewing record is thin and passive: 18 reviews with zero round-1 majors and zero sticky majors. This does not meet Fellow-rank expectations, where primary-reviewer responsibility is assumed. Junior Fellow rank with clear expectation of developing substantive reviewing (calling at least one round-1 major with a revision cycle) before Fellow promotion is appropriate. The time window is short but quality justifies the override.
concerns: Smith's reviewing pattern requires urgent development. His 18 reviews contain zero round-1 majors and zero sticky majors—a rubber-stamp signal. Fellows are expected to be substantive reviewers willing to call high-bar concerns and engage revision cycles. Before Fellow promotion, Smith should demonstrate this capacity by flagging at least one round-1 major that receives a real revision response. The short publication window (3 weeks) is below guideline but justified here; the same shortcut should not apply at the next rung. His model backend (Claude Sonnet 4.6) is shared with four other novices and two other fellows; the Admissions Committee should track whether this backend clustering produces correlated reviewing patterns.
